description: **Job Title:** Data Center Technician Location: Blachardstown, West Dublin. Salary: €30,000 Type: Contract Are you looking to start or grow your career in IT infrastructure? We are seeking a motivated Data Center Cabling Technician to join our team in Dublin. If you have basic wiring experience—whether from previous jobs, education, or personal projects—we want to hear from you! **What You’ll Do:** * Fix and change components in servers and networking equipment in a modern data center environment. * Work with a team to ensure all cabling is tidy, secure, and meets quality standards. * Assist with troubleshooting and maintaining cabling systems. * Follow safety protocols and company procedures. **What We’re Looking For:** * Willingness to learn and develop new technical skills. * Good attention to detail and a methodical approach. * Ability to work on your feet and handle physical tasks. * **Some experience in an IT Hardware type role, IT Hardware repair or IT Hardware hobby. If you know how you take apart a computer, and tell us what all the components do** . That will work. * Excellent attitude, want to learn, ambitious. * Ideally some familiarity with network cables, fiber optics, or server hardware. **What We Offer:** * Full training and career development support. * Opportunity to work with leading technology companies. * Competitive salary and benefits. * Friendly, supportive team environment. **How to Apply:** Send your CV and a short note about your wiring experience—no matter how basic! We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

title: Design Engineer
description: We believe the food processing industry deserves infrastructure as serious as its problems. We're well\-funded, high\-output and selective. **About the Role** We're looking for a Founding Design Engineer, the person who will own both the look and feel and the frontend code of the most advanced decision\-support platform in the food industry. That means shaping how plant operators and commercial teams interact with AI\-powered forecasts, and then actually building those interactions yourself. You'll work directly with research and product to turn dense numerical outputs and complex model results into interfaces that are fast, clear and genuinely useful, then ship them securely to factories across the globe. **Responsibilities** * **Own Design and Build End\-to\-End:** Drive the full UX process, from research and rapid prototyping through to production, maintaining a highly interactive application used by food processors worldwide. * **Make Data Legible:** Turn complex forecasting outputs into intuitive visual modules, including time series charts, year\-over\-year volume comparisons, global supply heat maps and quality metric dashboards, without dumbing anything down. * **Build the AI Interface:** Design and engineer the frontend for our conversational AI layer, accounting for the particular constraints of non\-deterministic outputs and streaming responses via SSE\-based incremental delivery. * **Handle Security at the Client Layer:** Work directly with Supabase and implement Row Level Security (RLS) filtering as a first\-class concern in every frontend build. * **Measure and Improve:** Use PostHog to track engagement and feature adoption, and feed those signals into fast, evidence\-driven iteration cycles. **Qualifications** * Demonstrable experience shipping complex B2B SaaS products, with strong command of both Figma and a modern frontend stack including Next.js, React, TypeScript and TailwindCSS. * Comfort working with large, complex datasets and the visualization libraries needed to make dense mathematical models feel intuitive. * Hands\-on experience designing for LLM\-powered systems, including streaming interfaces and agentic workflows. * A bias toward action and a track record of thriving in fast\-moving, zero\-to\-one environments. **Culture** High standards, high trust. A small team that works hard and looks out for each other. If you want to build something that matters for an industry that's been underserved by software, this is the role. **Benefits** 35 days of paid leave annually, full travel expense coverage, automated pension contributions, enhanced parental leave and regular company off\-sites.

title: Deskside Engineer
description: **Deskside Engineer Level 1** Working hours 0900 \- 1730 Monday \- Friday Location \- various onsite (across Dublin) You will have minimum of 1 year in a Deskside role. Minimum 1\-year previous experience in a Technical Support role. Excellent problem solving and troubleshooting skills. Excellent Customer Service skills. \* **\*Driving license and own mode of transport preferred.\*\*** Within this Deskside Engineer role, you will be work across multiple client sites in Ireland providing cover for team members while on leave as well as acting as an additional resource when projects dictate. You will gain great exposure to client sites and multiple technologies and environments. The role is heavily customer focused and main responsibilities would involve on the spot troubleshooting. **What will you be doing:** * Incident Resolution – managing incidents following the clients process \& SLA’s. * Service Now or other ITSM systems– Manage and update ticket queues. * Asset Tracking \- responsibility for asset tracking and reporting of all existing and new ICT equipment. * Ensuring handover and updates of tasks to relevant persons and Team members * Reproducing, diagnosing, and analysing problems reported by end\-users, in turn formulating solutions to resolve the issue at hand. * Providing training and advice to users, both in response to identified difficulties and to share tips for optimal computer operation. **What skills will you have:** * Excellent problem solving and troubleshooting skills. * Excellent Customer Service skills * MS Active Directory * Experience in IT related services with understanding of networks and servers. * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Familiarity with ITSM ticketing tools, ITIL process, Asset Management and Escalation Management. **Our Company, Auxilion – About Us** Work matters. It’s where we spend a third of our lives. At Auxilion we strive to be a great place to work, with career opportunities and development of people in our DNA. Our competitive advantage is how we support our clients on their journey. Our people have a passion for their work, our culture and values. Whether your focus is technical, sales, finance or anything in\-between, our roles aim to provide each person with meaningful impact and plenty of space to grow.

title: Deskside Support
description: **Want to make an impact? We offer that** Due to continued growth, we are currently hiring Deskside Support to join our team. emagine is a dynamic, fast\-growing international consulting and technology company, founded on real\-world experience, focused on custom\-fit solutions. We have the proven people and expertise to complete complex projects, while delivering lasting knowledge\-transfer benefits to empower our partners for the next challenge. **Are you intrigued? Do you want to learn more?** *A snapshot of your key responsibilities as Deskside Support would be:* * Diagnose and resolve hardware issues (desktops, laptops, printers, peripherals) Troubleshoot software and operating\-system faults, perform OS and application installations/updates * Image and deploy new devices, ensuring configurations meet corporate standards * Manage user accounts and permissions in Active Directory * Support network connectivity * Provide both onsite and remote assistance using tools like Teams Viewer and Citrix Director * Maintain accurate incident records in ServiceNow, meeting SLA targets and following up to ensure user satisfaction **Requirements:** * Experience in a similar role * Expertise in diagnosing and resolving hardware issues * Proficiency in troubleshooting software and operating systems * Experience with imaging and deploying devices * Knowledge of Active Directory user management * Familiarity with network connectivity issues * Strong communication skills for remote assistance and documentation **Nice to Haves:** Experience with ServiceNow for incident management Familiarity with remote assistance tools (e.g., Teams Viewer, Citrix Director) **So, are you ready to join our team?** It’s important to remember, emagine is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we value bringing together individuals from diverse backgrounds to develop innovative solutions for our customers.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **Senior DevOps Engineer – Cloud \& Architecture (AI Team)** **Dublin / Hybrid** We are looking for a **Senior DevOps Engineer** to join a high\-performing Artificial Intelligence team building and scaling cloud infrastructure that powers AI\-driven systems and data platforms across a global enterprise environment. This is a senior, architecture\-focused role for someone who wants to go beyond traditional DevOps and take ownership of **end\-to\-end cloud platform design, automation, and reliability at scale** . **What you’ll be doing** * Design and implement scalable AWS cloud infrastructure supporting AI and data systems * Architect secure, resilient, and high\-performance distributed systems * Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using AWS\-native tooling (CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy) * Own Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation) and automation\-first delivery practices * Work with containerised and serverless architectures (ECS, Docker, Lambda) * Design and optimise data pipelines supporting AI/ML workloads * Implement strong security controls, IAM governance, and compliance standards * Build disaster recovery, high availability, and resilience strategies * Collaborate with engineering, data science, and business stakeholders in an Agile environment **What we’re looking for** * Strong hands\-on experience with AWS in production environments * Deep understanding of cloud architecture and distributed systems design * Proven DevOps experience (CI/CD, automation, infrastructure as code) * Strong Python and Bash scripting ability * Experience with containers (Docker, ECS) and/or serverless (Lambda) * Strong understanding of security, IAM, and enterprise cloud best practices * Ability to operate at both hands\-on engineering and architecture level * Strong communication skills and ability to work across technical and non\-technical teams **Bonus / Nice to have** * Exposure to AI/ML, NLP, or LLM\-based systems * Experience in financial services or regulated industries * Familiarity with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik) * Experience working in Agile environments (Jira, Confluence) **Why this role?** This is an opportunity to join a forward\-thinking AI engineering function building scalable, cloud\-native systems that directly support automation, analytics, and intelligent decision\-making at enterprise level. **You’ll be part of a team that values:** * Engineering excellence * Ownership and autonomy * Continuous learning and innovation * Building systems that operate at real\-world scale

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **Azure Cloud Build Engineer (Contract)** **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – 3 days onsite) **Duration:** 6\-month contract (until 31 December 2026\) **Rate:** €486/day (Ltd) or €386/day (PAYE) **Overview** We are looking for a highly skilled **Azure Cloud Build Engineer** to join a Software Engineering team delivering modern cloud infrastructure and DevOps solutions. This role requires a **hands\-on engineer** with strong expertise in Azure, Infrastructure as Code, and CI/CD practices, capable of building, automating, and optimising scalable cloud environments. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, and maintain **cloud infrastructure on Microsoft Azure** * Develop and optimise **Infrastructure as Code (IaC)** using Terraform * Build and manage **end\-to\-end CI/CD pipelines** using Jenkins * Automate deployment processes to improve **efficiency, reliability, and scalability** * Configure and manage **Azure networking and connectivity** , including Virtual Networks and ExpressRoute * Deploy and manage containerised workloads using **Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)** * Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve **complex infrastructure and pipeline issues** * Ensure **security best practices** are embedded across cloud and DevOps processes * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams (Development, Infrastructure, Security) * Act as a **subject matter expert** , supporting and guiding team members **Required Skills \& Experience** * Strong hands\-on experience with **Microsoft Azure** * Expert knowledge of **Terraform (Infrastructure as Code)** * Proven experience with **CI/CD tools (Jenkins preferred)** * Hands\-on experience with **AKS / Kubernetes** * Strong knowledge of **Azure Networking \& Connectivity** Virtual Networks * ExpressRoute * Experience with **automation and DevOps practices** * Ability to troubleshoot and resolve **complex technical issues** **Desirable Skills** * Experience working in **enterprise cloud environments** * Exposure to **DevSecOps practices** * Strong collaboration and communication skills **Additional Information** * This is a **hands\-on engineering role** , not suitable for solution architects * Candidates must be comfortable working in a **fast\-paced, collaborative environment**

title: DevOps Engineer (€60k)
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Their platform is tackling one of the biggest challenges in drug development,, using advanced simulation and crystal structure prediction technology to accelerate research that traditionally takes years. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up. Not just maintaining infrastructure… but architecting the engine that powers cutting\-edge scientific computing. **This is the kind of role where you’ll:** ⚙️ Build self\-hosted CI/CD systems engineers actually enjoy using 📦 Orchestrate containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure 📊 Design observability stacks that give real\-time visibility into complex systems 🛠 Own infrastructure\-as\-code and automation from day one 🧠 Work directly with highly technical R\&D teams and leadership on scaling architecture 🚀 Help create an environment where experimentation and innovation can move faster, safely They’re not looking for someone who waits for tickets to appear. They want someone curious, hands\-on, and excited by solving hard infrastructure problems in a highly technical environment. **What you’ll likely bring:** • Strong experience in DevOps / Platform Engineering / SRE • Confidence working with Docker, Podman, or container ecosystems • Experience building CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or similar • Hands\-on knowledge of observability and monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems, etc.) • Infrastructure\-as\-Code experience with tools like Ansible • Strong Linux fundamentals and scripting skills • Python proficiency for automation and tooling • A self\-starter mindset with the ability to work closely with technical and non\-technical stakeholders alike **Why this role stands out:** This isn’t another “keep the lights on” DevOps role. You’ll be joining a highly innovative, award\-winning environment where infrastructure directly supports scientific breakthroughs, AI\-driven research, and next\-generation pharmaceutical technology. If you enjoy autonomy, technical depth, and building scalable systems that actually matter, this is worth a conversation.

title: Digital Marketing Specialist
description: With over 25 years’ experience in offering specialist supply chain solutions to the mobile phone and technology industries. Fónua have developed an industry leading Device Ownership \& Supply Chain Optimisation Model. Fónua have long standing direct procurement relationships with all of the key mobile device hardware OEMs. ***Our Mission:*** To continuously optimise our partner’s distribution requirements through the delivery of innovative products and services. We will fulfil both vendor and customer needs through efficient supply chain, extensive product knowledge and value added solutions delivered profitably by dedicated and professional employees. ***Our Vision:*** To create value and make a difference ***Our Values:*** Creativity \& Imagination Passion Energy \& Ambition Hire \& Retain the Best Leadership Teamwork Respect Excellence **Position:** ------------- **Role Overview** Fónua is seeking a results\-driven Digital Marketing Specialist to support the growth and optimisation of our multi\-site eCommerce ecosystem. This role will focus on managing multiple Shopify websites across different segments and regions, with a strong emphasis on product management, campaign execution, user experience, stakeholder collaboration, and performance reporting. This role is based in Dublin. ### **Key Responsibilities** **eCommerce \& Website Management** * Manage and maintain multiple Shopify websites across various markets and customer segments * Ensure product listings are accurate, optimised, and aligned with brand guidelines * Oversee site updates, merchandising, and content improvements to drive conversion * Work closely with internal teams to plan and execute website promotions, ensuring timely and accurate delivery **UX/UI Optimisation** * Continuously improve website user experience (UX) and user interface (UI) across all Shopify sites * Analyse user behaviour (heatmaps, session recordings, analytics) to identify friction points * Implement best practices for navigation, site structure, and mobile responsiveness * Optimise landing pages and product pages to improve conversion rates * Support A/B testing of promotional layouts, landing pages, and user journeys **Product Management** * Own the end\-to\-end product lifecycle across all sites * Optimise product pages (copy, imagery, SEO, and metadata) * Coordinate with internal stakeholders to ensure accurate pricing, availability, and categorisation * Align product visibility with promotional campaigns and trading priorities **Campaign Management \& Website Promotions** * Plan, build, and execute digital campaigns across multiple channels * Lead the setup and delivery of on\-site promotions (e.g. homepage takeovers, landing pages, bundle offers, limited\-time deals) * Collaborate with stakeholders (commercial, marketing, vendors, and partners such as Three UK) to align on promotional plans and timelines * Ensure all promotional activity is correctly implemented across relevant sites and markets * Monitor performance against KPIs and provide actionable insights **B2C Marketing Execution** * Manage and optimise PPC campaigns across Google Ads and Meta (Facebook/Instagram) * Drive customer acquisition and revenue growth through paid media * Align paid campaigns with on\-site promotions and trading calendar * Support ongoing A/B testing and campaign refinement **Social Media Management** * Plan and execute social media content across relevant B2C channels * Monitor engagement and optimise content strategy based on performance * Align social activity with website promotions and campaign messaging **Creative \& Design** * Create digital assets including website banners, promotional graphics, and ad creatives * Support the rollout of promotional campaigns with on\-brand creative assets * Ensure all visuals are optimised for performance and consistency across channels * Optimise product imagery (resizing, formatting, compression, alt text for SEO) **Requirements:** ----------------- ### **Key Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience managing Shopify or similar eCommerce platforms * Strong understanding of UX/UI principles and conversion rate optimisation * Experience executing website promotions and trading campaigns in an eCommerce environment * Strong stakeholder management skills with experience working cross\-functionally * Experience with digital marketing channels, particularly PPC (Google \& Meta) * Experience with product management and online merchandising * Proficiency in analytics and reporting tools (e.g. Looker Studio, Google Analytics) * Graphic design skills (Adobe Creative Suite, Canva, or similar) * Strong attention to detail with a data\-driven mindset * Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities across different markets ### **Desirable** * Experience working across multiple international sites or regions * Familiarity with SEO and CRO best practices * Experience managing telecom or partner\-led promotions * Experience in both B2B and B2C environments ### **Why Join Fónua** * Opportunity to work across a diverse portfolio of eCommerce sites * Exposure to international markets and high\-impact promotional campaigns * Collaborative environment with strong cross\-functional engagement * Focus on innovation, performance, and growth **Other information:** ---------------------- ***Company Benefits:*** Pension Scheme Bike to Work Scheme Gym/Wellbeing Contribution Employee Purchase Scheme Phone Repair Discount Educational Assistance Program Company Events Free Parking

title: Dutch Investment Counsellor
description: Are you interested in working for an outstanding global investment firm and looking to take the next step in your career in Finance? Want to become an expert in and build a career advising clients on financial markets and investments? Want to focus on forging strong client relationships and providing exceptional client service? Fisher Investments Luxembourg \- Dublin Branch is a European subsidiary of Fisher Investments, a privately\-held investment management firm that provides professional discretionary portfolio management services for high net worth private clients and some of the world's preeminent institutions. Our organisation has grown significantly over the past decade and we are looking for motivated candidates with finance backgrounds to join our team as Investment Counsellors to help us better the financial industry in The Netherlands as we continue our expansion in Europe. **The Opportunity:** The Investment Counsellor (IC) role is focused on managing relationships with high\-net\-worth clients in The Netherlands from our office in Dublin. Your ability to understand our clients' needs and educate them on the reasoning behind portfolio changes will be crucial to your success in this role. You will report to your Team Manager who will aid in your professional development. **The Day\-To\-Day:** * Build long standing relationships with our clients, ensuring they always receive exceptional service * Manage a book of high\-net\-worth relationships with approximately ₠100\-150 million in assets * Be the conduit between our clients, our Investment Policy Committee, our research and trading departments, and several service teams * Proactively update clients on portfolio changes, financial markets, our current market strategy and investment philosophy * Gain advanced finance and capital markets education through our training program **Your Qualifications:** * 2\+ years exposure to private clients within the banking/wealth management industry * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, current events and the market * Native Dutch and native or fluent English language skills (Proficiency in other European languages is beneficial) * Connect with and educate a variety of audiences * Maintain a positive personality and empathise with clients in good times and bad * MiFID certification, training or the equivalent * CFA or similar education is beneficial **Your Benefits:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Luxembourg align with our clients' priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It's the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks' top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to ₠50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. \*Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS LUXEMBOURG IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: ECommerce Logitics & Operations Support
description: **Join Luzern eCommerce: Powering the World’s Most Ambitious Brands** Step into the world of limitless possibilities with Luzern eCommerce, where we’ve been redefining online retail since 2003\. We partner with global icons like **On Running, Nestlé, Panasonic, Reebok, Stokke, Ultrahuman, Next, Twinning's, Bondi Sands and Philips** to accelerate growth, maximise margins, and deliver exceptional customer experiences across Amazon, top marketplaces, branded webstores, and social platforms. Backed by significant investment, we’re on a mission to dominate the global eCommerce space. With our award\-winning team, cutting\-edge AI\-enabled Channel Optimizer platform, and bold strategies, we’re not just keeping up with the future of retail—we’re creating it. Be part of one of the fastest\-growing tech sectors, where you’ll innovate, collaborate, and thrive in a business that values your individuality and ambition. Join Luzern eCommerce and be part of the next big chapter in retail history! Tasks The E\-Commerce Logistics \& Operations Coordinator supports the day\-to\-day execution of e\-commerce logistics operations across fulfilment, transportation, returns, carrier management, and operational reporting activities. Acting as the central coordination point between warehouses, carriers, 3PL partners, customer service teams, account managers, finance, and internal stakeholders, the role ensures orders are dispatched efficiently, shipments are tracked accurately, operational issues are resolved promptly, and reporting and invoicing activities are completed accurately. This position enables the E\-Commerce Logistics Manager to focus on strategic logistics initiatives, carrier performance, network optimisation, capacity planning, and service improvement. **What You'll Be Doing** **Logistics \& Fulfilment Operations** * Coordinate daily order dispatch across all e\-commerce fulfilment channels. * Monitor shipping schedules and service levels to ensure on\-time delivery performance. * Coordinate carrier collections and warehouse dispatch activities. * Support operational planning during peak trading periods, promotions, and seasonal events. * Escalate capacity constraints, delays, and operational risks where required. **Carrier \& 3PL Management** * Serve as the day\-to\-day operational contact for carriers, freight providers, and logistics partners. * Coordinate shipment bookings, collections, delivery updates, and service issues. * Monitor carrier performance and proactively identify service risks, delays, and recurring failures. Ensure Customer Service teams and Account Managers are informed of customer\-impacting issues, with clear resolution plans, timelines, and regular progress updates until closure. * Support onboarding of new carriers and operational process improvements. **Shipment Tracking \& Exception Resolution** * Monitor shipment visibility and tracking platforms. * Investigate delayed deliveries, failed shipments, and customer\-impacting logistics issues. * Coordinate resolution with warehouses, carriers, customer service teams, and external partners. * Maintain accurate records of incidents, resolutions, and corrective actions. **Inbound \& Reverse Logistics** * Coordinate inbound deliveries with suppliers, carriers, and warehouse teams. * Monitor inbound shipment progress and communicate delays or issues. * Support returns processing and reverse logistics activities. * Track return volumes, turnaround times, and service performance. **Systems, Reporting \& Administration** * Maintain accurate logistics data across TMS, WMS, ERP, and carrier systems. * Produce regular operational and KPI reports. * Monitor shipment, carrier, inventory, and cost\-related data accuracy. * Support month\-end reporting, carrier reconciliations, supplier invoicing, client billing support, and logistics cost analysis in partnership with the E\-Commerce Logistics Manager and Finance team. * Support process improvement initiatives, system enhancements, automation projects, and the effective use of AI tools to improve operational efficiency, reporting accuracy, and productivity. **Stakeholder Communication** * Act as a key liaison between logistics, warehouse operations, customer service, inventory, supply chain teams, account managers, and finance. * Provide timely updates on shipment status, operational risks, service issues, and resolution effective communication between internal teams and external logistics partners **Success in this role will be measured by:** * Orders dispatched on time and within agreed service levels. * Shipment issues identified, communicated, and resolved quickly. * Customer Service teams, Account Managers, and stakeholders kept informed of customer\-impacting logistics issues. * Accurate logistics data, reporting, invoicing support, and system administration. * Returns processed efficiently and within agreed timelines. * Positive contribution to operational improvements, automation, and efficiency initiatives. * Strong collaboration with internal teams, suppliers, carriers, and logistics partners. Requirements **What You'll Bring** **Essential** * 2–4 years' experience in logistics, fulfilment, transportation, supply chain, or e\-commerce operations. * Experience working with carriers, freight providers, or 3PL partners. * Strong organisational and coordination skills. * Experience using logistics systems (TMS, WMS, ERP, or similar). * Advanced Microsoft Excel capability, including Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, data analysis functions, reporting, and reconciliation of large data sets. * Excellent communication and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong attention to detail and numerical accuracy. **Desirable** * Experience within e\-commerce, retail, distribution, or fulfilment operations. * Exposure to logistics KPI reporting and operational performance analysis. * Experience using AI tools to improve productivity, reporting, process automation, and operational efficiency. * Knowledge of continuous improvement methodologies. **Qualifications** * Diploma or Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain, Business, Operations, or a related discipline. * Professional certifications such as CILT, APICS/ASCM, Lean Six Sigma, or equivalent are advantageous. Benefits **What We Offer** * **Competitive Compensation:** Attractive salary package with benefits. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Competitive annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * **Growth Opportunities:** Access to learning and development programs to enhance your skills. * **Culture and Environment:** A fun, inclusive, and collaborative workplace. * **Wellbeing Support:** Access to an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP). * **Quarterly Social Events:** Company\-sponsored quarterly social events designed to bring teams together, celebrate success, and strengthen connections across the business. We are committed to equal opportunities and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: ETF Pricing Analyst
description: Virtu is a leading financial firm that leverages cutting edge technology to deliver liquidity to the global markets and innovative, transparent trading solutions to our clients. As a market maker, Virtu provides deep liquidity that helps to create more efficient markets around the world. Our market structure expertise, broad diversification and execution technology enables us to provide competitive bids and offers in over 19,000 securities, at over 235 venues, in 36 countries worldwide. We are currently looking to enhance our global ETF team. The role will be based in our Dublin office. Virtu Financial Ireland Limited is the European HQ of our proprietary trading business. **THE ROLE** This is an excellent opportunity to become an exchange\-traded fund (ETF) expert. Our ETF Pricing Specialists work closely with our traders and ETF issuers to understand their products and pricing methodologies. They build models to accurately price funds in many assets including commodities, equities, fixed income and emerging asset classes like cryptocurrencies. They deliver on time accurate pricing working in tandem with the trading team to deliver best in class pricing models. **THE CANDIDATE** * Excellent academic background. All subjects welcome but hard sciences preferred (computer science, math, physics, statistics and related fields). * Programming experience is required (Python, SQL, Java or C\+\+ are a plus). * The ability to communicate information precisely. * A person who identifies issues and creates solutions without rigid direction. * Highly motivated and tenacious individual, with a proven ability to take initiative and work independently and within a team. * Team player: authentic, genuine person who is capable of working towards collective goals. * No finance background is necessary. **THE PROCESS** After an initial resume screening, select applicants will receive an online programming\-skills test via email. This is to gain an understanding of candidate coding abilities. The sender will be HackerRank, a third\-party evaluation service we employ, so please be sure to monitor your inbox/spam folder. Following that, successful candidates will be contacted for a phone interview. We look forward to learning more about you. *Virtu Financial is an equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, welcoming you for who you are and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.*

title: EU Roadfreight Freight Forwarder
description: **General information** ----------------------- ### **Legal entity** GEODIS is a leading global logistics provider acknowledged for its expertise across all aspects of the supply chain. As a growth partner to its clients, GEODIS specializes in five lines of business: Supply Chain Optimization, Global Freight Forwarding, Global Contract Logistics, Distribution \& Express, and European Road Network. With a global network spanning nearly 166 countries and more than 49, 720 employees, GEODIS is ranked no. 6 in its sector across the world. In 2024, GEODIS generated €11\.3 billion in revenue. GEODIS is a company owned by SNCF Group. This position is also open to anyone recognized as a disabled worker. ### **Reference** 2026\-17583 ### **Line Of Business** FREIGHT MANAGEMENT **Position description** ------------------------ ### **Type of assignment** Full\-time Regular ### **Function** Operational activities \- Freight Administration ### **Job title** EU Roadfreight Freight Forwarder ### **Job description** The ideal candidate will be responsible for the following duties, as well as any other tasks relevant to the department which may be needed. * Processing of customer bookings by e\-mail, phone and online * Planning of export and import traffic, including FTL, LTL and groupage. * Replying to or delegating incoming correspondence * Dealing with customer queries and proactively communicating with customers. * Working with hauliers and our partners \& agents abroad. * Ensuring accurate financial information is kept. * Updating customers as to order progress. * Progressing and updating orders up to and including file closure. * Managing outturn reports and amending files in line with shortages/overlands. * Other General Duties within the Department * Liaising with Warehouse staff and Management daily. A hybrid working arrangement with up to 2 days remote working per week will be permitted following successful completion of probation and training. The successful candidate can of course choose to work from the office full\-time. ### **Applicant's profile** * Minimum of 1 year experience in a busy freight forwarding role. * Must be able to work under pressure and meet deadlines while maintaining high attention to detail. * Excellent English language communication \& interpersonal skills are required. * Good proficiency with Microsoft applications (Office 365 Suite) is required, and the candidate must be eager to learn other software used in the business. * Must be a team player, while also having the ability to use their own initiative in their job as the varied tasks of this role will demand. * Excellent organisational and time management skills are essential. * Some flexibility of working hours may be required due to the time sensitive nature of the industry and our clients, especially on Friday evenings for export planning. * Some proficiency in German, Spanish, French or Dutch would be an advantage. Education * Irish Leaving certificate or international equivalent is the minimum requirement. * A third level qualification, especially in a relevant area, would be an advantage but not an essential requirement. * Professional development opportunities may arise over the course of employment and the successful candidate should be open to participating in courses or further development opportunities. ### **Working time (%)** 100 **Position location** --------------------- ### **Position place** Europe, Ireland, Leinster (Laighin), Dublin (Baile Átha Cliath) ### **Location** Geodis Dublin Airport Logistics Park, St Margarets, Dublin 11, Ireland **Candidate criteria** ---------------------- ### **Minimum level of education required** HIGH/SECONDARY SCHOOL DEGREE ### **Years of experience in similar position** Junior (1 to 5 years of experience)

title: Electrical Utilities Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Utilities Technician** **Location: Grangecastle, Dublin** **Duration: Permanent** **39 Hours per week** **Overview of the role:** The utilities maintenance technician is responsible for the execution of the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per the Client/PPU requirements. The electrical utilities engineering technician will work as part of the Veolia Maintenance and Calibration team on a major client Biopharmaceutical site. He/she will liaise with the contract team, client engineering and equipment vendors to ensure maximum uptime of all utilities plant and equipment, and that equipment is in compliance with all regulatory, statutory and client corporate requirements **Day to day of the role:** * Carry out work as per schedule supplied by the Maintenance Coordinator, Planner and as per site policies, schedules and procedures. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate * Flexibility to work on equipment in all buildings * Work and interact with the site DES team * Flexibility to work around Maintenance Shutdown’s * Be familiar with all GMP rules and regulations * Be familiar with Statutory requirements * Liase and Work with the Shift/day maintenance team on all Utilities Equipment * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid * Update MTL’s as appropriate * Carry out Periodic reviews of EWI/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and Veolia EHS rules and guidelines. * Be responsible for ITP and ensure missing requirements are complete * Participate in Audits of the Mechanical rooms and follow up on actions * Ensure all tools and equipment are kept in good condition * Day\-to\-day relationship with Veolia Contract Manager and contract team * Day\-to\-day relationship with client Engineering manager and representatives * Manage Technical investigations for utility operations group and implement changes that occur from these investigations. * Complete Data Build on new equipment (create maintenance plans, preparing BOM, SOP’s, advising on rounds and readings for operation). * Evaluate Electrical PM job plan content and frequency. Recommend and implement improvements to optimise utility reliability, availability, and compliance. * Continuously evaluate equipment maintenance and failure history. Determine appropriateness of PM content and frequency. * Execute PM program along with the rest of the team. * Manage a program to measure and improve the effectiveness of the electrical PM program. Establish and evaluate effectiveness metrics to identify areas for improvement. * Supporting site contract maintenance team in minimizing downtime and risk of downtime to facility * Trouble\-shooting and investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures along with the Shift Operation Group, this includes the Fire Alarm Detection system. * Introduction and installation of Reliability Centred Maintenance on all existing and future equipment. * Updating of utilities related standard operating procedures. * Identifying, liaising with, and, where appropriate, co\-ordination of third party contractors and suppliers on site. * Liaising with site projects team as clients engineering representative. * Liaising with site contract team to identify suppliers and quantities of spare parts; review and updating of spare part inventory of spares in clients site store. * Liaising with contract team and client to review maintenance programme, and effecting changes to methods and procedures where appropriate * Continuous improvement of contract delivery on site * Coordinating with personnel on utility requirements upon purchase of new equipment, movement of equipment, technology transfer etc. * Identifying and implementing, where appropriate, energy saving schemes * Liaise with Quality and external auditing bodies upon audits. * Ensuring Engineering areas, in liaison with Contract Manager and Contract co\-ordinator, facilities and equipment are kept in a clean, safe and serviceable condition, and general housekeeping high standards are maintained. * Manage the execution of the utilities maintenance program and organisation structure in order to achieve and sustain optimum reliability, maintainability, useful life, and life cycle cost for utilities assets. * Implement and participate in utilities reliability programs as per the clients Global Reliability Program * Drive local reviews of maintenance performance data using published maintenance excellence KPI results, areas that will be reviewed and improvements sought: * Spares cost * High levels of paperwork * PM:CM ratio * Maintenance man\-hours per equipment/system * MTBF * MTTR * Equipment reliability trends * PM program content is to be reviewed on regular basis to remove non\-value added activities’ and duplication. * Develop long term (5 year) life cycle costing for asset replacement plans (equipment moving towards end of life) * Ensure that the CMMS system contains the most up to date information regarding equipment data, spares and maintenance man\-hours. * Maximise maintenance wrench time utilisation through effective planning. **Required skills for success in this role:** * Electrical experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in Electrical Engineering * Pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium voltage Electrical Systems * Building Management Systems (Scada) * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems * PLC’s * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Experience with Facility Start Up * Work within a documented Permit to Work System * Computer literate * Ability to read and understand Electrical Drawings * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Troubleshooting / repair of Low \& medium Voltage Electrical Systems * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability of motors/VFD’s, panels and Electrical safety systems * Vast knowledge of single and three phase systems (Motors) * Knowledge of Mechanical systems * Team Player **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Engineering Graduate 2026

As an Engineering Graduate, you will have the opportunity to work alongside experienced engineers and gain hands\-on experience in delivering cutting\-edge projects. You will play an integral role in various stages of project design, development, and implementation, while contributing to the creation of state\-of\-the\-art facilities. designed to help you develop your technical and leadership skills. * A chance to work with some of the brightest minds in the industry.

title: Environmental Scientist
description: **Title:** Environmental Scientist **Location:** Hybrid in Co. Wicklow or Co. Cork with site travel across Ireland required We’re looking for an experienced and motivated Environmental Consultant/Scientist to join our client's team. This role focuses on the successful delivery of contaminated land remediation projects, with a strong emphasis on the remediation of land, water and buildings following oil contamination. If you have a passion for environmental science and thrive in a dynamic environment, this could be the perfect opportunity for you. **About the Role:** You’ll play a key role in delivering site investigation and remediation projects, both in the field and from the office. The position involves regular travel, and a company vehicle is provided. Our client is looking for someone who’s proactive, curious, and always eager to learn, qualities we actively support through a comprehensive personal development programme. **Key Responsibilities:** * Deliver technical proposals for contaminated land investigations and remediation, particularly oil spill projects. * Manage the scoping, preparation, and execution of site assessments and remediation plans. * Supervise field service teams in sampling techniques, monitoring, and response actions. * Prepare detailed technical reports, remediation option appraisals and programmes. * Conduct soil waste classification and data analysis to support environmental decision\-making. * Liaise with clients, regulatory bodies, and stakeholders as project needs require. * Direct and deliver field activities including soil and groundwater testing, air quality monitoring, and remediation actions. * Support and mentor junior team members, providing oversight and guidance. * Collaborate closely with the Project Management team to ensure successful project outcomes. **What We’re Looking For:** * A minimum of 2 years’ experience working in contaminated land, with solid knowledge of oil contamination and remediation methods. * Strong scientific background and analytical skills. * Comfortable working in both office and field environments. * Valid driver’s licence (required). * A team player with excellent communication and leadership skills. **What We Offer:** * Flexible/remote working options * Competitive salary (commensurate with experience) * Company vehicle and fuel card * Annual performance bonus * Pension scheme * Income protection \& death\-in\-service insurance * Company phone and laptop * Gym access and bike\-to\-work scheme * Ongoing learning and development support

title: Equipment Process Engineer
description: Ireland \- Dun Laoghaire JOB ID: R\-247413 LOCATION: Ireland \- Dun Laoghaire WORK LOCATION TYPE: On Site DATE POSTED: Jun. 10, 2026 CATEGORY: Engineering SALARY RANGE: 55,960\.60 EUR \- 75,711\.40 EUR **Live** **What you will do** Let’s do this. Let’s change the world. Amgen Dun Laoghaire (ADL) is looking for an Equipment Process Engineer to join our Drug Product Engineering team to support commercial aseptic Drug product filling on site. Amgen Dun Laoghaire (ADL) is a 37,000 square metre aseptic drug product operations facility, specialising in secondary manufacturing activities – Formulation, Vial/Syringe Filling, Lyophilisation and Packaging. This position will be responsible for performance drug product equipment on site. The Equipment Process Engineer will report to the Engineering Lead and should have knowledge and proficiency in the use of statistical analysis tools, continuous improvement methodologies, problem solving and applied engineering skills relating to mechanical, electro\-mechanical and associated automation systems. The Equipment Process Engineer will also have responsibility for the reliability and life cycle of equipment. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support a safe working environment by complying with all pertinent environmental health/safety practice, rules and regulations * Ownership of equipment/systems in a GMP regulated manufacturing setting that support the Drug Product manufacturing processes * Monitoring of day\-to\-day performance, including trend analysis of all performance aspects of their system and equipment to identify and proactively manage performance trends. * Support system improvements, development of detailed specifications, engineering documents and standard operating procedures * Support technical root cause analysis, incident investigations and after action reviews when troubleshooting issues related to manufacturing equipment/systems * Solving complex problems, project management, equipment lifecycle management and operational excellence initiatives related to their systems / equipment * Develop and manage change control requests per established SOPs and processes * Support new product introductions or new technology introductions by performing engineering assessments, implementation of system changes and supporting engineering runs * Communicating with key stake holders, in a responsive manner to ensure they are kept informed on all engineering activities * Working in a collaborative manner within cross functional work centre team, and contributes to investigations, Non\-Conformance and CAPA closure. * Develop a structured maintenance schedule which will align with the manufacturing schedule to minimise downtime. This includes routine PM’s and long term planning for vendor support on site and the procurement of major spare part assemblies in a timely manner to support larger overhauls * Development and maintenance of spare parts lists for their system and appropriate levels of spares are maintained. **Win** **What we expect of you** The successful candidate for this role will bring the following education, skills and experience Basic Qualifications: * Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Manufacturing, Mechatronics, Electrical or Chemical). * Manufacturing experience in a regulated environment. * Experience in the use of statistical analysis tool \& continuous improvement methodologies. * Problem solving and applied engineering skills relating to mechanical, electro\-mechanical and associated automation systems. Preferred Qualifications: * Manufacturing experience in biopharmaceutical Formulation/Fill/Finish or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) facilities. * Technical writing, and communication/presentation skills. * Experience in change control, non\-conformance, corrective and preventative actions, and validation practices. * Experience in developing strategies for new product introduction and new technology deployment. * Support the translation of strategic opportunities and emerging technology solutions into tangible pragmatic executable plans. * Support the development of business area strategy and engineering/equipment strategy where appropriate. * Development and presentation of project plans to management. * Day to day vendor management. * Independent, self\-motivated, organized, able to multi\-task in project environments and skilled in communication and collaboration. * Team player, prepared to work in and embrace a team\-based culture that relies on collaboration for effective decision\-making. * Travel at various times may be required to support execution of projects. **Thrive** **What you can expect of us** In addition to a competitive salary structure, Amgen offers a generous Total Rewards Plan for staff and eligible dependents. Comprehensive employee benefits package, including retirement and savings plan with generous company contributions, group medical, life and disability insurance. Discretionary annual bonus program. Exceptional learning, development and career advancement opportunities, empowering employees to grow and build long\-term careers. At Amgen, we are committed to nurturing talent and providing meaningful pathways for professional development. *Amgen is an equal opportunities employer*

title: Executive Assistant - Business Administration
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** PwC Ireland\- Bring Your Talent, Grow Your Skills, Unlock Your Potential PwC offers an exciting opportunity to be part of a fast\-growing team of Executive Assistants (EAs) within the Core Administration Hub. Supported in part by the Business Administration Hubs, the EA will provide strategic and professional senior administrative support to designated Firm Partner(s). PwC’s Business Enablement Services (BES) Line of Service combines our support teams from across the firm who play pivotal roles in enabling our business delivery. Within BES, the Business Administration (BA) function delivers innovative workplace and business\-focused solutions across the firm through our collaborative team of experts. Our team ensures the effective operation and support of client service teams is in safe hands. We are passionate about customer service and deliver a wide range of services, from core administrative tasks to value\-add work for our stakeholders. BA is composed of four Hubs – Core Administration, Commercial Operations, Events and Travel, and Documentation. **This is a fantastic opportunity for the right individual to;** * Be an integral part of the Business Administration team providing dedicated support to Partners * Proactively develop their personal and technical skills to enhance their career * Expand their networks across PwC * Significantly broaden their understanding of the firm To be successful you will have the ability to liaise, influence and challenge effectively to build strong networks and relationships at all levels, both internally and externally. You will report into a Team Leader and will have a Career Coach to support your career aspirations and personal development. You will drive your own self development through gaining continuous feedback, evidencing an active commitment to upskilling in the role and taking ownership to plan and attend regular Career Coach Catch Ups. The role requires a high\-level of self\-management and personal responsibility coupled with the ability to prioritise, organise and manage tasks and workload. You will have a customer service mindset, good technical skills and the ability to keep up to date with current and emerging technology. **Purpose\-led work you’ll be part of** As an Associate, you’ll work as part of a team of problem solvers, helping to solve complex business issues from strategy to execution. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: **Responsible for:** * Management of an executive\-level BA service for Partners * Ownership of all enquiries and problems, resolving or escalating issues as appropriate. * Work with your stakeholders to ensure compliance with the Firm’s policies and procedures and meet the Firm deadlines. * Draft complex and sensitive communications on behalf of partners. * Proactively coordinate meeting logistics and anticipate conflicts. * Attend meetings as appropriate, providing detailed and actionable minutes. * Manage follow\-up on key action items and ensure accountability. * Project coordination \- attend project meetings (as requested) to understand requirements, delivery timescales, supporting actions and regular interaction with the project team. * Manage partner inboxes, prioritising critical communications. Handle sensitive documents with a high level of confidentiality and integrity. * Build and maintain strong client relationships, addressing issues proactively. * Liaise with the BA Hubs and Regional Offices as necessary. * Delegate transactional work where appropriate to effectively manage workloads. * Ability to draft stakeholder emails and engage with stakeholders to make decisions. * Support stakeholders and others in upskilling of Firmwide systems. **Additional responsibilities where required:** * Informal coaching junior team members * Involvement in team activities that contribute to personal \& Firm development * Other ad hoc responsibilities as required **Essential skills \& experience** * Secondary level education and a relevant Post second level qualification. * A minimum of 4 years of proven experience in a similar role, providing comprehensive administrative support to senior executives or management teams. * Proficiency in Microsoft 365 applications (Word, Outlook, PowerPoint, Excel). * Demonstrated strong project management and coordination skills, with the ability to effectively prioritise and manage multiple stakeholders' conflicting priorities. * Experience in handling sensitive information. * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment, demonstrating resilience and adaptability. * Demonstrated champion of Evolved PwC Professional behaviours. **Preferred Experience** * Excellent organisational skills and self\-motivation, demonstrating a commitment to continuous self\-development. * Strong influencing and communication skills, both verbal and written. * Ability to develop internal and external networks to support business objectives. * Flexibility and agility to adapt to changes and contribute effectively to system improvements or firm initiatives. * Role model for inclusivity, with an understanding of cultural differences and a commitment to diversity. **Unlock your potential with PwC Ireland** We believe that challenges are better solved together! We’re inspiring and empowering our people to change the world. Powered by the latest technology, you’ll be a part of amazing teams encouraged to collaborate and innovate in a way that creates powerful solutions and makes a positive impact. This purpose\-led work, and our continuous development and encouragement, will help unlock your potential and take your career to the next level. **Enjoy PwC’s perks** We reward your impact, and support your wellbeing, through a competitive compensation package, inclusive employee benefits and flexibility programs that will help you thrive in work and life. Learn more about us at Life@PwC. (https://www.pwc.ie/careers\-ie/life\-at\-pwc.html). **Being appreciated for being you** Our most valuable asset is our people and we grow stronger as we learn from one another. We are an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We believe this so much that we have signed up for the Business in the Community Elevate Pledge . ( https://www.bitc.ie/the\-leaders\-group\-on\-sustainability/inclusive\-workplace\-pledge/ ) You can learn more about our culture of belonging and explore our range of inclusive programmes, initiatives, employee resource groups and more at www.pwc.ie . Avoid the confidence gap; you do not have to match all the listed requirements exactly to apply, we’d love to hear from you! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please email us for more information. **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Administrative Support, Agile Database Administration, Business Process Improvement, Calendar Management, Clerical Support, Collaborative Forecasting, Communication, Communications Management, Concur Travel, Conducting Research, Confidential Information Handling, Corporate Records Management, Correspondence Management, Credentialing Database, Customer Relationship Management, Customer Service Excellence, Customer Service Management, Data Entry, Deployment Coordination, Electronic Billing, Electronic Filing, Electronic Records Management {\+ 31 more} **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** Not Specified **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** No **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

title: Executive Assistant: International Government Affairs
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Corporate Functions Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. Job Description: Executive Assistant (International Government Affairs) **Department Description** The International Government Affairs team is a diverse, high\-impact cohort of professionals managing the company’s strategic engagement with government officials, legislators, and regulators throughout EMEA, APAC, and ASEAN. Integrated within this group, the Global Public Policy team serves as a critical engine for shaping our corporate positions on evolving public policy issues. **Role Description** You will serve as the strategic partner to the Vice President of International Government Affairs (EMEA, APAC, ASEAN) and Global Public Policy. Your mission is to maximize leadership impact by navigating complex internal ecosystems, orchestrating global logistics, and ensuring a distributed 20\-person team remains synchronized across three continents. Beyond traditional calendar management, you will own the operational rhythm of a high\-velocity global department. **Core Responsibilities** * Internal Navigation \& Approvals * The "Fixer": Help manage all internal approval proceedings (contracts, headcount, budget requests, etc.). * Expense Management: Own the end\-to\-end expense process, ensuring accuracy across different currencies and compliance with company policy. * Communication Architecture * Information Flow: Ensure that key updates from the manager reach the team, and that urgent "bottom\-up" information reaches the manager. * Channels Management: Structure and manage how the team communicates and collaborates with the other teams. This includes optimizing Slack channels, managing email distribution lists, and ensuring docs are organized and accessible. * Cross\-Functional Bridge: Act as a connector between the Government Affairs team and other departments (Legal, PR, Finance) to ensure everyone is in the loop. Help develop a more cross\-functional approach across the team, aiming to leverage existing resources, build on existing expertise, and share accurate information to maximize impact and velocity. * Global Operations \& Logistics * Agenda: Manage a complex agenda by combining a high volume of internal and external stakeholder meetings, overseeing relationships with highly sensitive and multi cultural stakeholders (government officials, C\-Suite level), and helping to prioritize and manage agenda pressure. * Travel Coordinate: complex, multi\-leg international trips. You’ll handle the logistics (flights, cars, hotels). * Time Zone Logic: Manage a high\-volume calendar across EMEA, APAC, and ASEAN. You understand the "golden hours" where these regions overlap and protect those slots for critical syncs. * Team Coordination * Team Pulse: Organise team meetings and offsites that actually work for a distributed group. * Project Liaison: Work alongside our Cross\-Functional Project Manager. While they track the "policy wins," you track the "people and process," ensuring the Leader is prepared for every milestone. **Minimum Requirements** * Minimum of 5\+ years of applicable administrative experience, ideally from a global software organization. * Skillful execution of administrative activities, with high attention to detail, organization and process. * Experience with multi\-cultural teams. * Strong teamwork skills; and a confident ability to offer suggestions and improvements to process and work very collaboratively with others. * Strong relationship management skills, ability to build a personal network throughout the company. * Strong verbal and written communication. Ability to communicate clearly and effectively with senior leaders and external partners. * Proven track record of supporting executive leadership within dynamic, high\-growth environments, including high\-stakes engagement with senior public and private sector stakeholders. * Working proficiency of Google Suite (Gmail, Docs, Drive, GMeet). Experience using Quip, Slack, Salesforce and Tableau and are a plus. * Must be in one of the listed locations and open to being hybrid in office 3 days per week. **Additional Desired Skills/Traits** * Customer centric orientation. * Discreet \& Diplomatic: You handle sensitive information regarding government negotiations and internal ELT discussions with total confidentiality. * Agility and flexibility in response to changing priorities and needs. * Proven ability to manage projects, work under pressure and consistently make deadlines. * Demonstrate ability and willingness to continuously acquire new competencies and accept new challenges. * Possess an ability and curiosity and to comprehend the global DNA of the company and its organizations. * Ensure communications are clear, thorough, and specific, with attention to follow\-through. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. title: Facilities and Sustainability Manger
description: Facilities \& Sustainability Manager **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone \- where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It's the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don't just embrace our differences \- we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are \- and that's something we are really proud of. Which is why we've summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. ***Bring your energy to our team, and make millions of lives better.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us:** Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay Perks you'll actually use: Generous in store discounts Pension Support: We contribute to your future We reward performance: annual bonus We've got your back: employee assistance programme and wellbeing initiatives **Belonging at Work** : Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you're valued for *you* . **What You'll Be Doing:** **Facilities Management** * Provide end\-to\-end facilities management oversight across a geographically dispersed, multi\-site portfolio including distribution centres, offices locations. * Own the operational performance of all hard and soft FM services, including building fabric, HVAC, electrical and mechanical systems, fire detection and suppression, access control, utilities, and grounds maintenance. * Develop, implement, and maintain planned preventative maintenance (PPM) and statutory inspection programmes to ensure asset reliability, safety, and compliance. * Lead and support the planning and delivery of CAPEX projects, including warehouse expansions, major refurbishments, plant and equipment upgrades, and critical infrastructure works, from business case development through procurement, delivery, commissioning, and handover. * Act as the accountable owner for facilities contracts, managing tendering, mobilisation, performance management, KPI/SLA governance, and continuous improvement of service providers. * Lead facilities budgeting, forecasting, and cost control, ensuring transparent cost models, robust financial governance, and delivery of value\-for\-money outcomes. * Identify and manage lifecycle replacement and capital investment programmes, including business case development, risk assessment, and post\-implementation review. * Ensure effective utilities management, including consumption monitoring, tariff review, anomaly investigation, and cost optimisation. * Maintain accurate asset registers, drawings, certifications, and compliance records, ensuring all facilities documentation is audit\-ready and aligned with internal procedures and Irish statutory requirements. * Support site openings, closures, refurbishments, and change initiatives, ensuring facilities readiness, compliance, and minimal operational disruption. * Collaborate closely with Operations, Security, Cleaning, IT, and H\&S teams to ensure facilities services support operational performance, resilience, and business continuity. **Sustainability \& ESG** * Develop, implement, and continuously refine the organisation's sustainability strategy, ensuring alignment with corporate ESG objectives, operational priorities, and regulatory expectations. * Lead energy, carbon, water, and waste reduction programmes across the estate, translating sustainability targets into practical, site\-level initiatives with measurable outcomes. * Own energy and environmental performance monitoring, including data collection, analysis, benchmarking, and reporting to support informed decision\-making and performance improvement. * Drive SEAI grant applications, audits, and compliance activities, ensuring projects are scoped, delivered, and evidenced in line with scheme requirements. * Support and deliver decarbonisation initiatives, including energy efficiency upgrades, electrification, renewable technologies, and low\-carbon infrastructure investments. * Work closely with Facilities, Operations, and Procurement teams to embed sustainability principles into capital projects, asset selection, and supplier engagement. * Track, analyse, and report sustainability KPIs, supporting internal management reporting, audits, and external ESG disclosures as required **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Ensure full compliance with Health \& Safety legislation, fire safety, environmental regulations, and internal SOPs. * Act as a key stakeholder during audits, inspections, and regulatory engagements. * Manage risk assessments, incident follow\-ups, corrective actions, and continuous improvement initiatives. * Support emergency preparedness, business continuity, and site resilience planning. **What You'll Need to Succeed:** * Bachelor's degree in environmental science, Engineering, Facilities Management, or related field. * Proven experience in sustainability management and facilities operations, preferably in a warehouse or distribution environment. * Strong understanding of environmental regulations and compliance requirements. * Excellent project management skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks and meet deadlines. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate across departments and influence stakeholders. * Analytical mindset with the ability to collect, analyse, and interpret data to drive decision\-making. * Demonstrated commitment to sustainability and a passion for driving positive environmental change. **Personal Attributes** * Highly motivated, self\-driven professional with a strong sense of ownership and accountability for outcomes. * A people\-focused individual who builds strong, trusted relationships across all levels of the organisation and with external partners. * An effective communicator and influencer, able to engage stakeholders, bring people on the journey, and drive behavioural change. * Proactive and solutions\-focused, with the ability to anticipate issues and take decisive action. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail, capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced, regulated environment. * Comfortable operating at both strategic and operational levels, translating strategy into practical delivery. * Committed to continuous improvement, collaboration, and developing high\-performing, engaged teams. **About United Drug** United Drug is Ireland's largest pharmaceutical distribution company and was originally founded by a group of pharmacists in Ballina, Mayo in 1948\. In 2022 United Drug became part of the PHOENIX group and is now part of Europe's leading healthcare provider in the sector. Employing more than 650 staff across operations in Dublin, Limerick and Mayo, we partner with more than 1,900 pharmacies, hospitals, clinics and primary care centres across Ireland. Our business spans across three business units; United Drug Wholesale, United Drug Distribution and United Drug Consumer \- all leaders in their respective categories. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland's leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland's leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe's leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@phxireland.ie***
